title: Western Blot Analysis
author: Unknown
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.iejcbcn

steps.instruction: Place cells in a microcentrifuge tube and centrifuge to collect the cell pellet.

Cells were lysed in RIPA buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA, 1% sodium dodecyl sulfate [SDS], 1% NP-40, 1:100 proteinase inhibitor cocktail, 50 mM β-glycerophosphate, 50 mM sodium fluoride). Transfer the supernatant to a new tube and discard the pellet，centrifugate 14000rpm 30min at 4°C. Test the concentration of supernatant, and prepare the protein denaturation.

Load up to 40 µl of sample to each well of a 1.5 mm thick gel. Guidelines for choosing gel percentages are based on protein size to be detected: 4-5% gel, >200 kD; 7.5% gel, 120-200 kD; 8-10% gel, 40-120 kD; 13% gel, 15-40 kD; 15% gel, < 20 kD
Transfer the proteins to PVDF membranes with a semi-dry transfer apparatus (Bio-Rad, Hercules, CA, USA) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.
The membranes were blotted with 5% dehydrated milk for 1 h and then incubated with primary antibodies overnight at 4°C with agitation.
Wash for 10 minutes with TBST three times .
Incubate the membrane for 1 hour at room temperature with horseradish peroxidase (HRP)‐conjugated secondary antibody, diluted to 1:5000 in 5% nonfat dry milk/TBST.
Wash for 10 minutes with TBST three times.

Incubate membrane (protein side up) with ECL (enhanced chemiluminescence substrate) for 1-2 minutes. The final volume required is 0.125 ml/cm 2 . Drain off the excess detection reagent, wrap up the blots, and gently smooth out any air bubbles. Place the wrapped blots, protein side up, in an X-ray film cassette and expose to x-ray film. Exposures can vary from 5 seconds to 60 minutes.
